Off Calendar Oct-28-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric McFarland appeared by zoom for Joanne Tybinka.
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for and with Brianna Blasko and Christine Wagner, and for John Pickering (Respondents).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to the issue of tolling and estoppel, whether Mr. McFarland's client's position constitutes a trust contest, whether or not 16061.8 applies, whether the court can rule on this by way of summary judgment, or demurrer/judgment on the pleadings, whether the document at issue is a protected document, whether or not the forgery was suppressed by the trustee, whether or not the 120-day time to respond has run, whether or not the trustee breached their fiduciary duty in providing notice to the beneficiaries when they discovered the writing, whether or not this has been brought in a timely manner, whether or not the moving party should be allowed leave to amend, the difference between civil procedures versus probate, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion for summary adjudication is granted (see tentative ruling for details). Mr. Cross points out two points that need to be corrected in the proposed order. Mr. McFarland's oral request to amend is denied.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eric McFarland appeared by zoom for Joanne Tybinka Blasko (Petitioner).
Robert White, Seth Skootsky and Robert Cross appeared by zoom for John Pickering and Christine Heyle, and for and with Brianna Blasko (by zoom) (Respondents/Trustees).

Mr. McFarland stated that he is not going to oppose the tentative ruling on the motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories no. 1 and 2 stating that he said everything he had to say in the papers. Counsel presented oral argument regarding the scope of the discovery request and the documents (paper/electronic) that are being requested, the process of producing beyond physical documents, the cost/expense of producing documents, whether or not there is anything to meet and confer about, etc. After hearing argument, the Court confirms the tentative rulings on the two motions to compel with a modification of 14-days to comply to 30-days. All timing as to discovery is 30-days. The parties are to meet and confer. The Court will prepare forms of orders to reflect the following:

1) Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Responses and for Sanctions is granted. Petitioner must produce non-privileged documents, and a privilege log if applicable responsive, to Special Interrogatories Number One and Two, within thirty days of entry of this order.
Sanctions are awarded to the Respondents and against Petitioner under CCP Sec 2023.030, in the amount of $2,460. Sanctions to be paid within fourteen days of entry of this order.

2) Petitioner's Motion to Compel production of documents in response to Amended Request for Production of Documents Set One is granted, pursuant to CCP Sec 2031.320. The production must comply with CCP Sec 2031.280. Respondents are directed to produce responsive, non-duplicate, non-privileged documents within thirty days of entry of this order. Absent agreement between the parties otherwise, a privilege log must be produced for any document withheld on the basis of privilege, within fourteen days of entry of this order.Respondents do not dispute that they are in possession of responsive non-privileged documents. There is insufficient showing that production of the relevant non-privileged electronically stored information would be from a source that is not reasonably accessible due to undue burden or expense. There is an inadequate showing that the projected potential cost of production is undue in consideration of the needs of the case, the amount in controversy, and the importance of the issues at stake in the litigation. Accordingly, the Court does not find good cause to allocate the expense of discovery under CCP Secs 2025.420(e).
Sanctions are awarded to Petitioner and against Respondents pursuant to CCP Sec 2031.320 in the amount of $2,460, as the Court does not find that Respondents acted with substantial justification in withholding the responsive non-privileged documents or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Sanctions to be paid within fourteen days of entry of this order.

3)Respondents' Motion to Quash Deposition Notice to Briana Anderson Blasko, for Stay of Deposition and for Protective Order is denied. Respondents have made an inadequate showing on which to find that the notice of deposition should be quashed. The Court declines to quash the notice of deposition. Respondents have not satisfied the burden established in CCP sec. symbol 2025.420 for issuance of a protective order regarding the production of non-privileged documents responsive to Petitioner's Amended Request for Production of Documents Set One. The Court declines to issue a protective order. It appears to the Court that the parties have spent an insufficient effort meeting and conferring regarding the discovery dispute prior to bringing the matter before the Court.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto by zoom, CSR#5229, 415-828-1919, p2gmoto@earthlink.net
